member.office_address: 423 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3226

form_analysis.form_header_text: Congressman Timothy Kennedy Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Members of the House have the opportunity to submit requests to the House Appropriations Committee for inclusion in annual appropriations bills. Congressman Tim Kennedy is proud to submit appropriate requests that support New York's 26th Congressional District. To submit an appropriations request, please fill out the following form. This form will allow you to submit one request at a time. Please note that the House Appropriations Committee has not yet released guidance on the FY27 process or deadlines. We will update the deadline for submitting requests to our office once that information becomes available.

sections.description: Please provide information for the individual and organization submitting this request.
Identify the relevant House Appropriations Subcommittee and the type of request being made.

fields.label: To submit your appropriations request, please indicate what Appropriations Subcommittee will consider the request.
fields.help_text: Congressman Kennedy submits requests to 12 specific subcommittees. Reference jurisdictional information for more details.
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food & Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health & Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing & Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Which category does your request fall under?
fields.help_text: Choose between Programmatic Funding, Bill Language, or Report Language requests.
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for the Science Mission Directorate, which is categorized as a programmatic funding request.

sections.description: Information about the federal program or agency related to your request.

fields.label: Federal Department (e.g. Department of the Interior)
fields.rationale: NASA is the parent agency for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Federal Agency (e.g. National Park Service)
fields.rationale: This is the specific component of NASA for which funding is requested.

fields.label: Program Name
fields.rationale: Standard reference name for this programmatic request as per 'Science' account guidance.

fields.label: Account Type (Defense Only - Procurement, RDT&E, etc.)
fields.rationale: This is a CJS/NASA request, not a Department of Defense request.

fields.label: Line Item (Defense Only)
fields.rationale: This is a CJS/NASA request, not a Department of Defense request.

fields.label: Program Element (Defense Only)
fields.rationale: This is a CJS/NASA request, not a Department of Defense request.

sections.description: Financial details and explanation of the community benefit.

fields.label: Program's Fiscal Year 2025 Funding Level
fields.help_text: Enter the numeric dollar amount enacted or provided for the program in FY25.
fields.rationale: Using the authoritative FY2025 Enacted amount from the reference values.

fields.label: Program's Fiscal Year 2026 Funding Level
fields.help_text: Enter the numeric dollar amount for the program for FY26.
fields.rationale: Using the authoritative FY2026 Enacted amount from the reference values.

fields.label: President's Budget Request
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released at this time.

fields.label: Your Funding Request
fields.rationale: This matches the 'Funding Ask' in the reference values.

fields.label: Explanation/justification of your request - please include how your request would benefit the 26th Congressional District of New York
fields.draft_value:
* Sustains U.S. global leadership in space science and counters rapid Chinese expansion in lunar and planetary exploration programs
* Supports over $2.59M in recent local economic activity across Western New York, providing a 3:1 return on federal investment
* Funds critical climate and biodiversity research at SUNY Buffalo and Daemen University, including leadership of the NASA sea-level change team
* Sustains high-tech industrial contracts for firms like Sandstone Springs in Niagara Falls for mission data restoration and Edwards Vacuum in Sanborn
fields.rationale: The justification highlights the strategic competition with China and the 3:1 ROI from the advocacy points. It specifically references local spending data ($2.59M), and lists specific institutions (SUNY Buffalo, Daemen University) and businesses (Sandstone Springs, Edwards Vacuum) in the district mentioned in the NASA spending context.

form_analysis.deadline_text: We will update the deadline for submitting requests to our office once that information becomes available.

top_grants.description: This project will combine Earth observations (E.g.  Ndvi from landsat and modis)  in situ observations  and ecological forecasting models to characterize the spatial and temporal variation of the vegetation state

top_grants.description: Development of modern optical navigation techniques for improved terrain relative navigation around small bodies
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced optical navigation for precise small body landings.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Research Foundation for the State University of New York
top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress. Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond. Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines. Develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: A strategic science agreement supporting lunar, Mars exploration, and applied research.
